MELItem.Title: Flight Deck Door Visual Surveillance Systems

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be inoperative and components may be missing provided:

ListItem.Para: Alternate procedures are established and used.
Repairs are made within three flight days.

Note.Para: Any portion of the system which operates normally may be used.

OperationProcedure.Para: Prior to flight, the pilot-in-command must brief cabin crew members that Flight Deck Entry Video Surveillance System is inoperative, and the following procedures to be used for requesting access to the cockpit.

ListItem.Para: Cabin crew requesting access to cockpit:
Request for access to cockpit is made using Interphone / Keypad.
Verification of request is made using Viewing Lens by the PM.

At least two crew members must be present in the cockpit throughout the entire flight. During the flight, if two flight crew members are in the cockpit and one of them leaves the cockpit, then a cabin crew member must occupy the cockpit jump seat.
For re-entry, the PM will request access to cockpit using Keypad.
Verification of request is made using Viewing Lens.
The cabin crew will unlock the door and exit cockpit when flight crew member returns.

DispatchCondition.Title: Flight Deck Door Viewing Port Operates Normally

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be inoperative and components may be missing provided:

ListItem.Para: Flight deck door viewing port operates normally.
Alternate procedures are established and used.
Interphone and Keypad systems operate normally.

Note.Para: Any portion of the system which operates normally may be used.

ListItem.Para: Entry password along with prearranged entry timing should be used to access the flight deck.
Where sufficient crew members are available on the flight deck, after the entry password is provided, the viewing port should be used to verify the identity of persons entering the flight deck.

DispatchCondition.Title: Procedures Do Not Require Its Use

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be inoperative and components may be missing provided procedures do not require its use.

ListItem.Para: Alternate procedures are established and used.
Repairs are made within three flight days.
Interphone and Keypad systems operate normally.

OperationProcedure.Para: Prior to flight, the pilot-in-command must brief cabin crewmembers, that flight deck Door Viewing Port and Electronic Visual Surveillance systems are inoperative, Interphone and Keypad systems operate normally, and the following procedures to be used for requesting access to the cockpit.

ListItem.Para: Cabin crew requesting access to cockpit:
Request for access to cockpit is made using Interphone and Keypad.
The code word for each flying duty period will be decided by the pilot-in-command and upon boarding the aircraft to brief the co-pilot and cabin crews.
The request for entry must be preceded by a decided code word.
When Flight crew leaves cockpit:
Prior to leaving the cockpit, the crewmember must verify the area around the cockpit is secured by communicating with cabin crew through the interphone system.
There is no requirement for a cabin crewmember to occupy cockpit jump seat when a flight crew member leaves the cockpit.
For re-entry, the PNF will request access to the cockpit using Interphone and Keypad preceded by a decided code word.
Video surveillance system is installed and operates normally.
Alternate procedures are established and used.

OperationProcedure.Para: Prior to flight, the pilot-in-command must brief cabin crewmembers, that flight deck Door Viewing Port is inoperative, and the following procedures to be used for requesting access to the cockpit.

ListItem.Para: Cabin crew requesting access to cockpit:
Request for access to cockpit is made using Interphone and Keypad.
Verification of request is made using the Flight Deck Entry Video Surveillance System by the PNF.
When Flight crew leaves cockpit:

Interphone System Operative: There is no requirement for a cabin crewmember to occupy cockpit jump seat when a flight crew member leaves the cockpit. For re-entry, the PNF will request access to the cockpit using Interphone / Keypad.

Interphone System Inoperative: At least two crew members must be present in the cockpit throughout the entire flight. During the flight, if two flight crew members are in the cockpit and one of them leaves the cockpit, then a cabin crew member must occupy the cockpit jump seat. For re-entry, the PNF will request access to cockpit using Keypad. Verification of request is made using the Flight Deck Entry Video Surveillance System.

Note.Para: For the Flight Deck Entry Video Surveillance System application use, one EFB must be operative. Refer to MEL 46-11-01-01 for (O) procedure.
